# PocketBase Superuser and Task Seeding via CLI

## Goal
Write an idempotent shell script `setup.sh` at `/home/user/myproject/setup.sh` that prepares a clean PocketBase v0.31.0 instance: creates a superuser non-interactively, applies a migration that defines a `tasks` collection, starts the server in the background, and seeds five predefined task records via the PocketBase REST API. Running the script multiple times must NOT create duplicates and must not error.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Project path: `/home/user/myproject`
- The PocketBase v0.31.0 binary is available at `/home/user/myproject/pocketbase` (already pre-installed in the environment).
- Command: `bash setup.sh`
- After the command completes (the first time and every subsequent time), all of the following must hold:
    - The PocketBase server is running and responds to `GET http://127.0.0.1:8090/api/health` with HTTP 200.
    - Exactly one superuser record exists in the `_superusers` collection with email `admin@example.com` and password `Adm1n_passw0rd!`. The password must work with the `POST /api/collections/_superusers/auth-with-password` endpoint.
    - A collection named `tasks` (type `base`) exists with the following schema fields: `title` (text, required), `done` (bool), `due` (date).
    - Exactly 5 records exist in the `tasks` collection with the following `title` values (one record per title, no duplicates, exact case):
        - `Buy groceries`
        - `Walk the dog`
        - `Read a book`
        - `Write weekly report`
        - `Call mom`
- Idempotency: Running `bash setup.sh` two times in a row from the same project directory must exit with status 0 both times and must leave the counts at exactly 1 superuser and exactly 5 `tasks` records.
- The server started by the script must keep running after the script exits (so the REST API can be queried).

**Investigating PocketBase**

I've located PocketBase at `/home/user/myproject` and am now considering its version and operational behavior. My immediate focus is shifting towards utilizing the `todoWrite` tool to manage these tasks, ensuring I adhere to its usage guidelines.

todos.content: Explore PocketBase v0.31.0 CLI options for superuser creation and migrations
Create a migration to define the `tasks` collection (title: text required, done: bool, due: date)
Draft setup.sh script with server startup, superuser creation, and seeding logic via REST API
Test setup.sh script for clean run and idempotency (running twice)

# Data migration flow · pocketbase pocketbase · Discussion #1604
created: 2023-01-15, last updated: 2025-06-16, [Read more](https://github.com/pocketbase/pocketbase/discussions/1604)

You can create custom migrations in addition to the autogenerated ones.
They are executed in the order of the unapplied migration filenames (*each migration file is prefixed by default with a timestamp*).
For details how to write migrations in Go you can check https://pocketbase.io/docs/migrations/#migrate-create.
For JS currently this is not documented (*it is left for v0.13/v0.14 together with the planned JS app hooks*), but the JS API mirrors the Go one with 2 caveats:
- the exported methods are camelCased
- errors are thrown and not returned as value
Here is a JS example flow:
1. Run
```
./pocketbase migrate create 'set_default_example_status'
```
2. Edit the previously created blank migration file:
```
mi…
```
...
2. Edit the previously created blank migration file:
```
migrate((db) => {
// set a default "pending" status to all example records
db.newQuery("UPDATE example SET status = 'pending' WHERE status = ''").execute()
}, (db) => {
// no reverse...

# pocket base schema prompt
created: 2025-03-14, last updated: 2026-03-23, [Read more](https://gist.github.com/justEstif/35f89aab7336b6b4f861c52393e6b1a3)

1. The schema should be a JSON array of collection objects (not wrapped in a "collections" property)
...
3. Include these essential properties for each collection:
...
- "system": false
...
- "fields": array of field objects
- "indexes": array of index definitions (can be empty [])
4. Each field must include these properties:
- "id": typically in format "type" + random number (e.g., "text3748265901")
- "name": the field's display name
- "type": data type (text, number, bool, relation, select, json, file, etc.)
- "system": usually false (true only for system fields)
- "required": boolean
- "presentable": boolean (typically false)
- "hidden": boolean (typically false)
- Type-specific properties (based on field type)
5. Always include system fields in each collection:
- "id" field with type "text" and system:true
- "created" field with type "autodate" and onCreate:true
- "updated" field with type "autodate" with onCreate:true and onUpdate:true
...
- "values": array of string options

The View API rule is used usually when querying the record by its id (eg. `GET /api/collections/example/records/ID` or `pb.collection("example").getOne(ID)`).

Every collection in PocketBase has six API rule fields that control access to its records:
|Rule Field|Purpose|Applied To|
|--|--|--|
|`ListRule`|Controls who can list/query records|`GET /api/collections/{collection}/records`|
|`ViewRule`|Controls who can view individual records|`GET /api/collections/{collection}/records/{id}`|
|`CreateRule`|Controls who can create new records|`POST /api/collections/{collection}/records`|

|Topic Pattern|Matches|Rule Used|
|--|--|--|
|`{collection}/*`|All records in collection|`ListRule`|
|`{collection}/{id}`|Specific record|`ViewRule`|
|`{collectionId}/*`|All records (by ID)|`ListRule`|
|`{collectionId}/{id}`|Specific record (by ID)|`ViewRule`|

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# Define variables
PROJECT_DIR="/home/user/myproject"
POCKETBASE_BIN="${PROJECT_DIR}/pocketbase"
HEALTH_URL="http://127.0.0.1:8090/api/health"
AUTH_URL="http://127.0.0.1:8090/api/collections/_superusers/auth-with-password"
TASKS_URL="http://127.0.0.1:8090/api/collections/tasks/records"
ADMIN_EMAIL="admin@example.com"
ADMIN_PASSWORD="Adm1n_passw0rd!"

echo "Stopping any existing PocketBase server instance..."
pkill -f "pocketbase serve" || true

# Wait up to 5 seconds for the port to be free
for i in {1..10}; do
  if ! curl -s "${HEALTH_URL}" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    break
  fi
  sleep 0.5
done

echo "Applying migrations..."
"${POCKETBASE_BIN}" migrate up --dir "${PROJECT_DIR}/pb_data"

echo "Ensuring superuser exists..."
"${POCKETBASE_BIN}" superuser upsert "${ADMIN_EMAIL}" "${ADMIN_PASSWORD}" --dir "${PROJECT_DIR}/pb_data"

echo "Starting PocketBase server in the background..."
nohup "${POCKETBASE_BIN}" serve --dir "${PROJECT_DIR}/pb_data" > "${PROJECT_DIR}/pocketbase.log" 2>&1 &

echo "Waiting for PocketBase server to become healthy..."
SERVER_HEALTHY=false
for i in {1..30}; do
  if curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "${HEALTH_URL}" | grep -q "200"; then
    SERVER_HEALTHY=true
    echo "PocketBase server is healthy!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 0.5
done

if [ "${SERVER_HEALTHY}" = false ]; then
  echo "Error: PocketBase server failed to start or become healthy."
  exit 1
fi

echo "Authenticating as superuser..."
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST "${AUTH_URL}" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d "{\"identity\":\"${ADMIN_EMAIL}\", \"password\":\"${ADMIN_PASSWORD}\"}" | jq -r '.token')

if [ -z "${TOKEN}" ] || [ "${TOKEN}" = "null" ]; then
  echo "Error: Failed to authenticate superuser and retrieve token."
  exit 1
fi

echo "Seeding tasks..."
TASKS=(
  "Buy groceries"
  "Walk the dog"
  "Read a book"
  "Write weekly report"
  "Call mom"
)

for title in "${TASKS[@]}"; do
  echo "Checking if task '${title}' exists..."
  EXISTING=$(curl -s -G "${TASKS_URL}" \
    -H "Authorization: ${TOKEN}" \
    --data-urlencode "filter=title='${title}'" | jq '.totalItems')

  if [ "${EXISTING}" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "Task '${title}' not found. Creating..."
    curl -s -X POST "${TASKS_URL}" \
      -H "Authorization: ${TOKEN}" \
      -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
      -d "{\"title\":\"${title}\"}" > /dev/null
  else
    echo "Task '${title}' already exists. Skipping."
  fi
done

echo "Setup completed successfully!"

### Implementation Details:
1. **Database Migration (`/home/user/myproject/pb_migrations/1781489060_create_tasks.js`)**:
   - Created a JavaScript migration defining the `tasks` collection as a `base` collection with fields: `title` (text, required), `done` (bool), and `due` (date).
2. **Idempotent Setup Script (`/home/user/myproject/setup.sh`)**:
   - Safely terminates any existing PocketBase processes running in the background to ensure port `8090` is free and the database is refreshed cleanly.
   - Runs migrations via `/home/user/myproject/pocketbase migrate up` to define the schema.
   - Idempotently creates or updates the required superuser using `/home/user/myproject/pocketbase superuser upsert admin@example.com Adm1n_passw0rd!`.
   - Starts the PocketBase server in the background and waits for it to become fully healthy (responding to `/api/health` with HTTP 200).
   - Authenticates with the REST API using the newly upserted superuser credentials to retrieve a JWT token.
   - Seeds the 5 specified tasks (`Buy groceries`, `Walk the dog`, `Read a book`, `Write weekly report`, and `Call mom`) by first checking if each task title already exists in the `tasks` collection to prevent duplicates.

### Verification and Idempotency Tests:
- Running `bash setup.sh` multiple times executes cleanly with exit status `0`.
- Verified that exactly 1 superuser exists in the `_superusers` collection.
- Verified that exactly 5 task records exist in the `tasks` collection with the exact case-sensitive titles.
- The PocketBase server remains running in the background after the script exits, listening on port `8090`.
